sound_type: roaring bull collision at breeding rookery: two adult male Steller Sea Lions (600-1,000 kg each) clashing chest-to-chest; continuous deep resonant roaring from both; heavy concussive body impact; foreflipper percussion; within rookery ambient of hundreds of animals vocalizing; sustained combat minutes
ecosystem: Rookery -- breeding colony on rocky coastal substrate; peak bull territorial conflict during summer breeding season (June-August)
season: Summer (breeding season; peak bull competition June-August)
conservation_status: Near Threatened (IUCN, eastern DPS: Washington/Oregon, recovering). Endangered (western DPS: Alaska/western Pacific).
milestone: OTARIIDAE MAXIMUM. E=8 = Otariidae ceiling in the engine. Largest eared seal (Eumetopias jubatus); largest individuals reach 1,000 kg; extreme sexual dimorphism. The bull collision is the maximum energy Otariidae event: largest species + most intense behavior + densest animal acoustic environment on PNW coast.

coordinates.description: Pacific Northwest distribution: San Juan Islands, Strait of Juan de Fuca, Olympic Peninsula coast, Race Rocks (BC), Point Grenville WA. Non-breeding haulouts in Seattle waterfront and Puget Sound. Range: California Channel Islands north to Gulf of Alaska, Aleutians, eastern Russia.

acoustic_data.vocalization_type: Bull roar: deep resonant vocalization, one of the loudest produced by any Pacific Northwest marine mammal. Collision: percussive body impact (concussive, felt at close range). Combined with rookery ambient: hundreds of animals simultaneously.
acoustic_data.mechanics: Two bulls at full weight (600-1,000 kg each) rearing on forefflippers and lunging chest-to-chest. Mane region (thickened neck skin/muscle) absorbs collision and bite force. Combat involves: chest impact, foreflipper strikes against substrate, neck biting. Duration: minutes.
acoustic_data.frequency_range: Bull roar: 200-1,500 Hz, primary energy 400-800 Hz. Collision impact: broadband percussion across full spectrum. Rookery ambient: continuous broadband from hundreds of animals.
acoustic_data.duration: Sustained combat: minutes per engagement. Extended combats possible. Rookery ambient: continuous for breeding season duration.
acoustic_data.e8_calibration: E=8: maximum Otariidae energy. Largest species + most intense behavior (full bull combat) + densest sustained acoustic environment on PNW coast = ceiling calibration.

nodes.title: Otariidae Maximum at E=8
nodes.description: The Steller Sea Lion bull collision is the maximum energy event in the Otariidae behavioral repertoire: largest species, most intense behavior, within the densest sustained animal acoustic environment on the PNW coast. E=8 is the correct ceiling for this family.

nodes.title: Rookery as Maximum Acoustic Environment
nodes.description: The Steller Sea Lion breeding rookery at peak breeding season is one of the highest sustained acoustic energy environments on the PNW coast -- hundreds of animals vocalizing continuously. The bull collision is the peak event within this environment, not an isolated occurrence.

cross_references.relationship: Previous Steller Sea Lion entry at E=7. Single bull vocalization. Degree 306 escalates to E=8: two bulls + full collision + rookery ambient.
Related Otariidae. Smaller species, lower energy. Family context.
E=6 + E=8 = E=14. Asymmetric. Ecology outweighs music. Compound display parallel: Grunge/Psych + bull vocalization+combat both carrying multiple functions.
